patches.find: despite all three sharing the same parent prediction (PROS-002: "aetheric vortex precession below threshold"). The distinction is not that WIN-006 lacks a mechanism claim in the parent — it does not; the dome names aetheric vortex for both the pre-1990 linear phase and the post-1990 acceleration. The distinction is in the type of language
patches.replace: despite all three sharing the mechanism framing from PROS-002 (raw-text/02-wins.txt:19-22 describes aetheric vortex precession below threshold and runaway above threshold for the whole pre-1990/post-1990 sequence). PROS-002 in the dome WIN list names WIN-007 and WIN-036 as its explicit outputs (raw-text/02-wins.txt:22: "→ WIN-007 + W010 (WIN-036)"); WIN-006 is listed as a standalone confirmation with no parent-prediction arrow, but it reports the pre-1990 linear drift that PROS-002 invokes as its pre-threshold backdrop — the mechanism label is therefore shared across WIN-006/WIN-007/WIN-022 even though the dome does not draw an explicit arrow to WIN-006. The distinction is in the type of language
patches.rationale: c6 Hole 1 (moderate): current text says 'all three sharing the same parent prediction (PROS-002)' but raw-text/02-wins.txt:22 shows PROS-002 only arrows to WIN-007/WIN-036, not WIN-006 or WIN-022. A defender can quote this verbatim. Fix cites dome source and acknowledges the analytical construction explicitly, closing the attack surface.

patches.find: the claimed 0.0466 deg/yr rate cannot be reproduced from the NOAA NP.xy dataset by any standard metric, so the assertion of a match dissolves on contact with data.
patches.replace: the claimed 0.0466 deg/yr rate cannot be reproduced from the NOAA NP.xy dataset under any of the three standard geometric conventions (great-circle arc, longitude-only at NMP latitude, latitude-only) — 0.0466 deg/yr converts to 1.0–5.2 km/yr depending on convention, while observed pre-1990 NMP drift runs 10–15 km/yr (NOAA historical records) — so the assertion of a match dissolves on contact with data.
patches.rationale: c6 Hole 2 (minor, carried from c5): 'any standard metric' is sweeping but unspecific; defender can demand which metrics were tested. Replacement names three standard conventions and gives the km/yr range explicitly.

patches.find: cannot be reproduced from NP.xy by any standard metric.
patches.replace: cannot be reproduced from NP.xy by any standard angular-to-linear convention.
patches.rationale: c6 Hole 2: same fix for tldr_verdict; more concise since the full detail is in detail_verdict_text.
